violations.explanation: Inline page.locator() with Elements container reference used in test body. According to the mapping report, pause_plan_link exists in OverviewPage and should be accessed as a direct property. The framework pattern for existing locators is direct property access (Pattern A), not page.locator(elements.X). This violates Prohibition 6: ZERO page.locator() in test function body.
Inline page.locator() with Elements container reference. Should use direct property access.
Inline page.locator() with Elements container reference. Should use direct property access.
Inline page.locator() with Elements container reference. Should use direct property access.
Inline page.locator() with Elements container reference. Should use direct property access.
Inline page.locator() with Elements container reference. Should use direct property access.

Inline page.locator() with Elements container reference. The locators in print_history_page_additions.py should be defined as direct properties in the page object, not accessed via page.locator(elements.X).

changes.description: Locators should be defined as direct properties in __init__ method, not as Elements class attributes. The framework pattern is: self.billing_cycle_period_card = page.locator('[data-testid=billing-cycle-card]')

fix_details.explanation: Both the test script usage and the page additions structure need correction

violations.current_code:
bar_color = complimentary_bar.evaluate("el => window.getComputedStyle(el).getPropertyValue('background-color')")
            framework_logger.info(f"Complimentary pages progress bar color: {bar_color}")
violations.expected_code:
bar_color = complimentary_bar.evaluate("el => window.getComputedStyle(el).getPropertyValue('background-color')")
            framework_logger.info(f"Complimentary pages progress bar color: {bar_color}")
            # Verify color is black (expected result from test case)
            assert 'rgb(0, 0, 0)' in bar_color or 'black' in bar_color.lower(), f"Expected black color but got {bar_color}"
violations.explanation: Step 11 expected result states 'Verify the Complimentary pages progress bar is filled with the black color'. The code extracts the color but does not assert it is black. This is only logging, not verification.

fix_details.assertion_code:
# Verify color is black (expected result from test case)
            assert 'rgb(0, 0, 0)' in bar_color or 'black' in bar_color.lower(), f"Expected black color but got {bar_color}"

violations.explanation: The create_new_ii_v2_account() method returns the page object, but reassigning it is unnecessary and violates the pattern. The page object is already in scope from PlaywrightManager context.

            common.send_rtp_devicestatus(printer_data.entity_id, printer_data.cloud_id, printer_data.device_uuid)
violations.explanation: The send_rtp_devicestatus() method does not return a value (returns None). Asserting it is not None will always fail. The method call itself is sufficient for print simulation.

            common.send_rtp_devicestatus(printer_data.entity_id, printer_data.cloud_id, printer_data.device_uuid)

violations.explanation: Tooltip visibility check uses 5000ms timeout which is too short. Standard timeout for expect() assertions should be 30000ms (30 seconds) per framework standards.
Tooltip visibility check uses 5000ms timeout which is too short. Standard timeout should be 30000ms.
Tooltip visibility check uses 5000ms timeout which is too short. Standard timeout should be 30000ms.
Tooltip text check uses 5000ms timeout which is too short. Standard timeout should be 30000ms.
Negative visibility check uses 5000ms timeout. While negative checks can use shorter timeouts, the standard is 30000ms for consistency. Also has inline page.locator() issue (already flagged in V009).

violations.current_code:
GeminiRAHelper.access(page)
            page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle", timeout=30000)
            GeminiRAHelper.access_tenant_page(page, tenant_email)
            page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle", timeout=30000)
            GeminiRAHelper.subscription_to_subscribed(page)
            page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
            page.reload()
            page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle", timeout=30000)

            GeminiRAHelper.verify_rails_admin_info(page, "Free months", "0")
violations.expected_code:

            GeminiRAHelper.verify_subscription_state_and_free_months(page, tenant_email)
violations.explanation: This is a complex sequence of Gemini Rails Admin operations that is repeated multiple times in the test. According to Checkpoint D15, admin/backend interactions should be encapsulated in helpers. This sequence should be extracted into a single helper method.

fix_details.description: Extract the Gemini RA verification sequence into a helper method
fix_details.current_structure: Multiple GeminiRAHelper calls with waits and reloads
fix_details.required_structure: Single helper method call that encapsulates the entire verification flow

violations.expected_code:
# Consider creating a helper method: GeminiRAHelper.verify_subscription_state(page, tenant_email, expected_state='paused')
            GeminiRAHelper.verify_subscription_state(page, tenant_email, 'paused')
violations.explanation: Another Gemini RA verification sequence that should be encapsulated in a helper method.

fix_details.description: Extract the Gemini RA state verification into a helper method

violations.current_code:
class Elements:
    """
    Additional locator strings for PrintHistoryPage.
    Merge these into the existing Elements class in pages/print_history_page.py
    """
    
    # Billing Cycle Period Card - Main Container
    billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-card']"
violations.explanation: The page additions file defines locators as class attributes in an Elements class, but according to the framework pattern shown in the mapping report and coding standards, locators should be defined as direct properties in the page object's __init__ method. The framework uses two patterns: (1) Direct properties: self.element = page.locator('selector') accessed as page_obj.element, or (2) Elements container for grouped locators accessed via page.locator(page_obj.elements.selector). However, the mapping report shows that existing locators in PrintHistoryPage use direct property access (Pattern 1), so new locators should follow the same pattern. The current structure will cause the test script to use page.locator(page_obj.elements.X) which violates Prohibition 6.

fix_details.description: Restructure locators to be defined as direct properties in __init__ method instead of Elements class attributes

violations.explanation: The locator selector is too specific. According to the generation summary, the plan_pause_info_text locator should have multiple fallback selectors: '[data-testid=plan-pause-info'], .pause-plan-info, .plan-paused-message'. The current definition only has the data-testid selector, which may not match the actual element on the page. This could cause the test to fail when checking for plan pause information visibility.

The locator selector does not match the generation summary specification. According to the generation summary, complimentary_pages_progress_bar should use selector '[data-testid=complimentary-pages-bar'], .complimentary-progress-bar' with fallback to class selector. The current definition uses 'complimentary-pages-progress-bar' which is inconsistent.

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Replace all page.locator(page_obj.elements.X) with direct property access page_obj.X. The framework pattern for existing locators is direct property access. After fixing the test script, restructure the page additions files to define locators as direct properties in __init__ method instead of Elements class attributes.

Restructure both page additions files to define locators as direct properties in the page object's __init__ method instead of as Elements class attributes. The correct pattern is: self.locator_name = page.locator('selector'). This will enable direct property access in test scripts.

Update locator selectors to match the specifications in the generation summary. Add fallback selectors for robustness. For example, plan_pause_info_text should be '[data-testid=plan-pause-info'], .pause-plan-info, .plan-paused-message' instead of just '[data-testid=plan-pause-info]'.

Add color verification assertions after extracting progress bar colors. Steps 11, 14, and 18 extract the background-color CSS property but only log it without asserting the expected color (black for complimentary, yellow for additional). Add assertions like: assert 'rgb(0, 0, 0)' in bar_color or 'black' in bar_color.lower(), f'Expected black color but got {bar_color}'

Fix incorrect method usage patterns: (1) Remove page reassignment from create_new_ii_v2_account() call, (2) Remove incorrect assertions on send_rtp_devicestatus() which returns None. The method call itself is sufficient for print simulation.

Consolidate multiple log statements per step into single informative log statements. Each step should have one log line that includes all relevant information. For example, combine color extraction log and step completion log into one: framework_logger.info(f'Step 11 completed: Complimentary pages updated correctly - color: {bar_color}, value: 6 of 10')

Update all tooltip-related expect() assertions to use standard 30000ms timeout instead of 5000ms. While tooltips may appear quickly, the framework standard is 30000ms for consistency and to handle slow network conditions.

Extract repeated Gemini Rails Admin operation sequences into helper methods. Create methods like: GeminiRAHelper.verify_subscription_state_and_free_months(page, tenant_email), GeminiRAHelper.verify_subscription_state(page, tenant_email, expected_state), and GeminiRAHelper.shift_time_for_tenant(page, tenant_email, days, force_billing).
Separate the combined Precondition 2 & 3 comment into two distinct comments, one for each precondition. This improves clarity and matches the test case structure.
Update module-level docstrings to clarify whether locators are new additions or updates to existing locators. This helps developers understand the merge operation.
